item.title: Nuclear Starburst Ring Simulations
item.description: Summary Below, are embedded .gif files for Simulations \( \texttt{A} \) and \(\texttt{A}_\star \) from Nguyen & Thompson, 2022. The first set are 2d density, temperature, velocity, and entropy slices. The second set are rotated 3d temperature-weighted renders. The temperature weighting colors the hot \( 10^7 \)K component red, and the cooler \( 10^4 \)K component blue. The cool component transparency is signifigantly lowered such that the warm-hot structrure is prominently rendered. These movies are also available on vimeo, the links can be found here: 2d Vimeo links: Simulation \( \texttt{A} \) | Simulation \( \texttt{A}_\star \) 3d Vimeo links: Simulation \( \texttt{A} \) | Simulation \( \texttt{A}_\star \)

item.title: Simulation Gallery
item.description: Here are links to different pages that contain both 2d and 3d simulation movies. On each page are embedded gif files as well as links to vimeo, which may be useful if the embedded video does not correct play on device. 1. Nguyen & Thompson, 2022 : Nuclear Starburst Ring Simulations

item.title: Contact
item.description: The best way to reach me is through email at: nguyen.1971@osu.edu. My office is M2025 in the Physics Research Building at the main campus of Ohio State University.

item.title: Hello hello, welcome.
item.description: A brief timeline: I was born in San Jose, California and moved to Gilbert, Arizona during elementary grade school. I then attended Arizona State University for my undergrad and then moved to Columbus, Ohio for the graduate phd program at the Ohio State University. I enjoy backpacking/hiking at national parks, badminton, and skiing. The picture above was taken at the one sad hiking destination in the flatlands of Ohio: Hocking hills. Below, is a picture I took while hiking the Iceberg Lake trail at Glacier National Park in summer 2021. Growing up in Arizona, I have been spoiled with a

item.title: Research
item.description: I work in theoretical astrophysics focused on the physics and phenomenology of galactic starburst winds. For numerical studies, I use the massively-parallel GPU code, cholla, on the Pitzer cluster at the Ohio Supercomputer Center to run high resolution 3-dimensional hydro simulations. Research interests: supernovae driven outflows hydrodynamic instabilities X-ray astrophysics Publications You can find my publications listed here on arXiv or sao/nasa ads. first author Nguyen, D. D., Thompson, T. A., Mass-Loading and Non-Spherical Divergence in Hot Galactic Winds: Implications for X-Ray Observations, 2021, MNRAS, 508, 4, arXiv:2108.07343, ads:2021MNRAS.tmp.2635N co-author Lopez, L. A., Mathur, S., Nguyen, D.

item.title: Welcome
item.description: Hello, my name is Dustin Nguyen and I am a fourth year PhD candidate at the Ohio State University. I work with professor Todd Thompson. I have information on myself, my research, CV, and contact on the right. Happy browsing!

item.title: CV
item.description: My CV can be found here. Last updated 22 April 2022.
